title: fix: h100-mfu-calculation
body: I was getting 104% MFU on h100 then i realized that MFU calculation might of been based on a100 312 tflops h100 is 989 tflops at bfloat16. nvidia claims 1,979tflops but that is for 2:4 sparsity.

step_plan.summary: Fix MFU reporting on H100 by updating `GPT.estimate_mfu` to normalize achieved FLOPs against an H100-appropriate BF16 dense peak (989 TFLOPS) rather than the A100 constant, preventing misleading >100% MFU values on H100.

steps.operation: Make MFU normalization device-aware by selecting an appropriate BF16 dense peak FLOPs constant (H100=989e12, default/A100=312e12) instead of always using the A100 value
steps.reason: The current MFU calculation hardcodes `flops_promised = 312e12` (A100 BF16 peak), which inflates utilization on faster GPUs like H100 (leading to >100% MFU). Selecting the peak FLOPs based on the detected CUDA device name (e.g., substring match on 'H100') corrects the normalization for H100 while preserving A100 behavior.
steps.side_effects: MFU numbers will change on H100 (and any other GPUs that match new heuristics). Device-name parsing is somewhat brittle across cloud variants (e.g., 'NVIDIA H100 SXM', 'NVIDIA H100 PCIe'); ensure substring matching is robust. No expected impact on training correctness, but logged metrics (console/wandb) will differ and may require updating baselines.
